Selector.noActor: No NPC on this scene yet
Selector.showActor:
Left click: Show / hide actor on scene
Right click: Change avatar display
Selector.showItem:
Left click: Show / hide item on scene
Right click: Change item display
Selector.focusActor: Focus on the actor on the scene
Selector.disabledHint: The display of PCs as well as NPCs is disabled

Settings.disableHint: Disabling should be discussed with the game leader, as it could be important for his or her game management.
Settings.tooltipsHint: Tooltips help the game master to find his way around the module, especially at the beginning.
Settings.changelogHint: The possibilities of the module as well as the changelog can be found here.
Settings.showChangelogHint: Should the changelog be displayed for game masters after updates?
Settings.keyToggleSceneActorsViewHint: Actors are hidden or shown on their own screen. Players can hide actors only temporarily, that is, as soon as the GM toggles an actor in, all actors are visible again.
Settings.keyToggleSceneActorsHint: The module is completely enabled or disabled for the scene.
Settings.rotateActorsHint: Actors on the play area can be rotated by click
Settings.battleActorsHint: ATTENTION: This feature requires at least DSA system v5.1.0. On battlemaps, one of the BattleActorsSelectors replaces the SceneActorsSelector during an active battle. This allows the players to have an immersive battle on another (TotM) scene without a battlemap, while the game master can still keep track with the help of the battlemap.
Settings.hoverNamesHint: If the name of an actor has been unlocked for the players, it is only displayed when the player is over the avatar with the mouse. This also applies to the interaction field for items.

Changelog.title: Yendor's Scene Actors: Features & Changelog
Changelog.currentReleaseHint: This release contains minor improvements or bug fixes. More detailed information can be found in the

Canvas.tooltipClickActor:
Left click: Show actor in focus
Right click: Alternative portrait view
Alt + left click: Rotate actor (if option enabled)
Canvas.tooltipClickItem:
Left click: Show item in focus
Right click: Alternative item view
Alt + left click: Rotate item (if option enabled)
Canvas.tooltipItemToInventory: Take item and add to inventory
Canvas.tooltipItemLocked: Item cannot be viewed or taken

notification.noCharacter: Dem Spieler muss ein Charakter zugewiesen sein.
